scoring.cap_note: Forensic cap applied: a high-severity earnings-quality flag caps the overall score at 55 regardless of category scores (see Red Flags).

rules.rationale: Margin level proxies pricing power and business model quality.
Stable margins indicate durable economics rather than one-off swings.
Scale correlates with resilience and institutional interest.
Leverage entering listing is the primary balance-sheet risk.
Short-term liquidity buffer.
Ability to service debt from operating profit.

rules.evidence: REVENUE CAGR 50.4% over the restated period
rules.rationale: Multi-year compounding, not just the latest pre-IPO year.

rules.evidence: PAT CAGR 54.5% over the restated period
rules.rationale: Multi-year compounding, not just the latest pre-IPO year.

rules.thresholds: every yoy step positive → full points
rules.rationale: Consistency separates trend from a single dressed-up year.

rules.evidence: Issuer P/E at the price band could not be extracted from the Basis for Offer Price chapter.
rules.rationale: Peer-relative pricing from the RHP's own mandated comparison table.
Sanity check independent of the issuer-chosen peer set.

rules.evidence: Needs both issuer P/E and positive PAT CAGR

rules.rationale: Skin in the game after listing.

rules.evidence: OFS ₹0 cr of ₹210 cr total (0%) — fresh capital ₹210 cr goes to the company, OFS to selling holders
rules.rationale: Exit-heavy offers raise nothing for growth; every manual analyst review leads with this.

rules.evidence: Our Promoters have agreed not to dispose, sell, transfer, charge, pledge
rules.rationale: Pledged promoter stakes transmit personal leverage into the stock.
Criminal matters against company/promoters/directors.

rules.evidence: No qualification language detected in the restated auditor's report
rules.rationale: Auditor reservations are the highest-signal governance flag in the document.

rules.evidence: No dividends declared in reported years
rules.rationale: A payout history modestly evidences real distributable cash.
Each finding is itemised in the Risks tab with its own evidence.

rules.evidence: Company claims: “…one of the top five…” — note this is the issuer's own characterisation
rules.rationale: Claims are recorded but explicitly labelled as company-sourced.
Returns on capital are the core of business quality compounding.
Returns on capital are the core of business quality compounding.

rules.thresholds: all reported years positive → full points
rules.evidence: CFO positive in 1/3 reported years (FY24: ₹-65cr, FY23: ₹2cr, FY22: ₹-7cr)
rules.rationale: Operating cash positivity streak.
Profit that never becomes cash is the classic earnings-quality failure.

rules.thresholds: FCF positive→10 · negative→3 (heavy investment phase is common pre-IPO)
rules.rationale: Negative FCF is contextual pre-IPO; persistent negativity matters more.

objects.purpose: 1. Funding of capital expenditure requirements of our Company towards purchase of machinery and
2. Repayment or prepayment, in full or in part, of all or a portion of certain outstanding borrowings availed by
Limited, for repayment or prepayment, in full or in part, of all or a portion of certain outstanding borrowings
its capital expenditure requirements towards purchase of machinery and equipment;
4. Funding inorganic growth through strategic investments and/or acquisitions; and
(1) The cumulative amount to be utilized towards funding of inorganic growth through strategic investments and/or acquisitions and general
corporate purposes shall not exceed 35% of the Gross Proceeds. Further, the amount utilized for our object of ‘Funding inorganic
growth through strategic investments and/or acquisitions’ shall not exceed 25% of the Gross Proceeds. The amount to be spent towards
general corporate purposes will be finalised upon determination of the Offer Price and will be updated in the Prospectus prior to filing
with the RoC. The amount to be utilized for general corporate purposes shall not exceed 25% of the Gross Proceeds.

reasoning: Issuer P/E at the price band could not be extracted from the Basis for Offer Price chapter.

top.title: Negative operating cash flow in FY24, FY22

flags.detail: Receivables/sales ratio grew 1.55x year-over-year (Beneish DSRI-style signal; >1.4 is a classic manipulation marker).
(PAT − CFO)/Total assets = 18.8%; reported profit substantially exceeds operating cash (strongest single manipulation predictor in Beneish's work).

verdict: Mixed fundamentals · Valuation indeterminate · moderate risk profile

meta.disclaimer: This is an automated document analysis for research and education. It is not investment advice, not a recommendation, and not a SEBI-registered research report. Scores reflect only information inside the uploaded prospectus and stated assumptions.

bull.text: Revenue CAGR of 50.4% and PAT CAGR of 54.5% over the restated period
EBITDA margin of 18.6% in the latest restated year

bear.text: CFO/PAT ratio of -1.08 in the latest year, with operating cash flow negative in FY24 and FY22 (positive in only 1 of 3 reported years: FY24: ₹-65cr, FY23: ₹2cr, FY22: ₹-7cr)
Free cash flow of ₹-98 cr (CFO ₹-65 cr minus capex ₹33 cr)
